DISCOVERY 302, AUG-24-2023. RE: DEFENDANT ERICA BOLANOS' MOTION TO COMPEL SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORRIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION DENIED. THE DEADLINES FOR DISCOVERY AND DISCOVERY MOTIONS HAVE LONG PASSED AS THE DATE INITIALLY SET FOR TRIAL IN THIS MATTER WAS MORE THAN A YEAR AGO. C.C.P. SEC. 2024.020. FAIRMONT INSURANCE COMPANY V. SUPERIOR COURT, 22 CAL.4TH 245, (2000). PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUSTRIES, INC. V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODUCTS, INC., 165 CAL. APP. 4TH 1568 (2008); COTTINI V. ENLOE MED. CTR., 226 CAL. APP. 4TH 401 (2014). SINCE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF AGREEMENT AMONG THE PARTIES TO EXTEND DISCOVERY DEADLINES PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SEC. 2024.060 AND THERE IS NO PENDING OR CONCURRENT MOTION TO REOPEN/EXTEND DISCOVERY AND DISCOVERY MOTION DEADLINES PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SEC. 2024.050, THE COURT HAS NO CHOICE BUT TO DENY THE MOTION. PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUSTRIES, INC. V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODUCTS, INC., 165 CAL. APP. 4TH 1568 (2008). THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE PRO TEM JUDGE AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN. CLERK: R. WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Special Interrogatorries And Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DEFENDANT ERICA BOLANOS' Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Special Interrogatorries And Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion denied. The deadlines for discovery and discovery motions have long passed as the date initially set for trial in this matter was more than a year ago. C.C.P. Sec. 2024.020. Fairmont Insurance Company v. Superior Court, 22 Cal.4th 245, (2000). Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008); Cottini v. Enloe Med. Ctr., 226 Cal. App. 4th 401 (2014). Since there is no evidence of agreement among the parties to extend discovery deadlines pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 2024.060 and there is no pending or concurrent motion to reopen/extend discovery and discovery motion deadlines pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 2024.050, the Court has no choice but to deny the motion. Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008).

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem Judge at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by David L. Hart Esq. for plaintiff(s) Abner Ramos, Lidia Godinez; Appearance by Claudia Lozano Esq. for defendant(s) Lucy Beasley. Appearance by Wilma J. Gray Esq. for defendant(s) Erika Bolanos. The jury trial date is continued to 10-16-23 per agreement of parties and there will be no further continuances due to scheduling conflicts. Judge Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk VWU; Not Reported. (610)
